pickup_verification_recap.v0_pre_flight: python -c "import json; print('fleet_paused=', json.load(open('tools/autonomy_budget/state.json'))['fleet_paused'])" Output: fleet_paused= True
pickup_verification_recap.v0a_branch: git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD Expected: chore/platform-extraction Observed: chore/platform-extraction
pickup_verification_recap.v0b_log: git log --oneline -3 Expected: 9d17e7f, ccdd82b, 3b9b1b8 (top three) Observed: 9d17e7f (HEAD -> chore/platform-extraction) docs(platform_separation): Guide instance 4→5 handoff -- Stage 2 mid-stage at item 2.4 ccdd82b docs(platform_separation): Stage 2 mid-stage EA handoff v3 -- item 2.4 partial (6 of 13 entrypoints), resume at file 7 (self_check.py) 3b9b1b8 docs(platform_separation): Stage 2 mid-stage EA handoff v2 -- items 2.1+2.2+2.3 complete (uncommitted), resume at 2.4
pickup_verification_recap.v0c_status: git status --porcelain Observed (verbatim): M docs/platform_separation/GUIDE_PROMPT.xml D "docs/platform_separation/Temp for responses/0.10 blocked on two corrections - 01_STAGE0_PREFLIGHT.xml" D "docs/platform_separation/Temp for responses/0.7 re-verify - 01_STAGE0_PREFLIGHT.xml" M pyproject.toml M tools/autonomy_budget/config.yaml M tools/autonomy_budget/config_loader.py M tools/autonomy_budget/tests/test_config_loader.py M tools/fleet_observability/credential_rotation_reminder.py M tools/fleet_observability/daily_digest.py M tools/fleet_observability/dashboard_maintainer.py M tools/fleet_observability/weekly_summary.py M tools/fleet_observability/welcome_back_digest.py M tools/gate_stale_cleaner/run_live.py ?? docs/platform_separation/ea_outputs/EA_COMPLETION_REPORT_STAGE2_ITEM_2.4_PARTIAL_FOR_GUIDE.xml ?? docs/platform_separation/ea_outputs/EA_COMPLETION_REPORT_STAGE2_ITEM_2.4_PARTIAL_FOR_LA.md ?? docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/ ?? tools/_project_context.py ?? tools/_vikunja_client.py ?? tools/autonomy_budget/projects/ ?? tools/tests/ Classification: - 11 M paths (10 inherited + GUIDE_PROMPT.xml from g5-1 LA housekeeping). NO regression. - 2 D paths (g5-1: LA "Temp for responses" -> "temp_for_responses" rename housekeeping). - 6 ?? paths (4 inherited new tools artifacts + 2 EA-3 completion reports + 1 new temp_for_responses/ directory). All expected per g5-1. No EA-3 violations. No drift.
pickup_verification_recap.v0d_test_path: Test-Path on 7 pending files (in v3 execution order): tools/autonomy_budget/self_check.py -> True tools/autonomy_budget/blarai_next_task_resolver.py -> True tools/gate_stale_cleaner/cleaner.py -> True tools/fleet_ops/diff_builder.py -> True tools/autonomy_budget/active_tasks.py -> True tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py -> True tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py -> True 7/7 True.
pickup_verification_recap.v0e_pytest: .\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tools/ --tb=short -q Tail (verbatim): tools\vikunja_mcp\bridge\tests\test_multi_writer.py ....... [ 96%] tools\vikunja_mcp\bridge\tests\test_scheduled_cowork_ea.py ..... [ 98%] tools\vikunja_mcp\tests\test_server_f5.py .... [100%] ============================= 280 passed in 2.23s ============================= Baseline 280 passed. Zero regression vs EA-3 close-out (280) and v2 baseline (280).
pickup_verification_recap.v0f_fleet_paused: python -c "import json; s=json.load(open('tools/autonomy_budget/state.json')); print('fleet_paused=', s['fleet_paused'], '| reason=', s.get('fleet_paused_reason'))" Output: fleet_paused= True | reason= platform separation v2 in progress No state.resume_fleet() call this session. Reason string preserved verbatim.
pickup_verification_recap.v0g_stash: git stash list Output: stash@{0}: On main: wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002 Stash entry byte-identical to predecessor handoffs (timestamp 20260424_013002 unchanged). DO NOT POP / DROP / APPLY. Will remain untouched through Stage 4 cutover.
pickup_verification_recap.overall_verdict: ALL 7 PICKUP CHECKS PASS. Ground truth matches v3 handoff + g5-1 LA-housekeeping prediction. Ready for Guide instance 5 review.

required_acknowledgments_carryforward_ve.intro: Per i1-2 dedup rule and Guide-#5 close-out: 23 inherited binding acknowledgments (16 pre-existing + 7 Guide-#4 g4-N) plus the 4 g5-N acknowledgments from Guide instance 5 remain in force through Stage 4 cutover. Total: 27. Restated verbatim below.

pre_existing_critical_carryovers.ack: Stage 2 produces exactly ONE commit (item 2.9). All intermediate work stays uncommitted.
Fleet stays paused throughout Stage 2. NEVER call state.resume_fleet(). Fleet remains paused through Stage 4 cutover.
No merge to main during Stage 2. Branch chore/platform-extraction is the only working surface.
stash@{0} (wake_launcher auto-stash on main) is NOT to be popped or applied.
No edits to CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, or any A1-A10 governance doc during Stage 2.
All Vikunja access flows through tools/_vikunja_client.py. list_tasks / search_tasks MUST receive int project_id. bool is rejected before int (cleanup-C3).
_project_context.resolve() is the single source for ProjectContext in every refactored entrypoint. Hardcoded C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI paths in entrypoints are a BUG.
V8 defaults preservation: pre-existing pytest discovery, fleet behavior on the BlarAI path, and CLI default behavior all unchanged when --project-root is omitted.
STATUS.md updates at item 2.10 are APPEND-ONLY. Do not edit prior Stage entries.

plus_guide_3.ack: V1-V7 + V8 verification block before closing handoff.

plus_iteration_1_acks.ack: V0a-V0g pickup verification block must be re-executed (and outputs reported) at the start of every successor session before substantive work begins.
All 10 acknowledgments above (9 from predecessor + 3 from Guide instance 3, deduped) are binding through Stage 4 cutover.
V1-V7 closure verification (per EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml) plus per-item V8 (defaults-preservation) must be executed and outputs included in the final closing handoff at Stage 2 completion.

If a successor approaches context exhaustion before item 2.9, it MUST emit a v3 (or higher) mid-stage handoff XML following the same schema as v1/v2, commit ONLY that file (docs-only commit), and stop. NO partial-commit refactor work. NO emergency single-commit attempts that omit pending items.

plus_guide_4_acks.ack: I will incorporate all 8 corrections into a re-emitted comprehension gate before any item 2.4 work begins.
I will use `-BlarAIRoot` (not `-ProjectRoot`) for all 7 PS1 parameters per v2 canonical signature pattern at 2.4.v2.

I will explicitly enumerate the 12 Python entrypoints (9 load_config callers from v2-1 + 3 v2.4.v2 additions) and the 7 PS1 scripts (with la_merge_approve at the correct `tools/scheduled-tasks/` path).
I will include `tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py` MCP handlers in item 2.4 audit and extend item 2.6 grep patterns per C4/C6 source citations.
If item 2.8 `$BLARAI_PID` preflight returns empty or any value other than 3, I will STOP and report to Lead Architect — no silent regex or yaml adaptation.
I will use descriptive risk labels (no numeric prefix overlap with AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW or verification-block IDs).
I will not begin item 2.4 work, create branches, or run mutation commands until the re-emitted gate is reviewed and explicitly authorized by Guide instance 4.

ack.topic: Pickup verification expectations updated for LA-side housekeeping

plus_guide_5_acks.ack: The v3 handoff's predicted ground state (10 M + 4 ?? Temp + 4 ?? new tools artifacts) does NOT include the LA-side housekeeping that landed AFTER EA-3 emitted: (a) modified docs/platform_separation/GUIDE_PROMPT.xml (+19 lines, v2.1 file-output-discipline carve-out); (b) deleted "docs/platform_separation/Temp for responses/0.10 blocked..." and "docs/platform_separation/Temp for responses/0.7 re-verify..." (LA's old-XML housekeeping); (c) renamed directory "Temp for responses" -> "temp_for_responses" (lowercase, underscores per v2.1 convention) -- visible in git as one new untracked entry "?? temp_for_responses/"; (d) two new ?? entries for EA-3's own report files (EA_COMPLETION_REPORT_STAGE2_ITEM_2.4_PARTIAL_FOR_GUIDE.xml + EA_COMPLETION_REPORT_STAGE2_ITEM_2.4_PARTIAL_FOR_LA.md). All FOUR additions are EXPECTED and not violations. EA-4's iteration-1 V0 pickup MUST account for these or it will report false drift.

ack.topic: File 12 path-label mismatch in EA reports vs v3 handoff

plus_guide_5_acks.ack: The EA's Guide-facing completion report (.xml) and LA-facing report (.md) both label file 12 as `tools/_vikunja_client.py` (the new chokepoint, Stage 2.1). The CORRECT path for the legacy load_config caller is `tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py`. The v3 handoff XML <files_pending> correctly uses `tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py`, so EA-4 (which reads the v3 handoff, not the LA/Guide reports) is not impacted -- the path is correct in the source-of-truth document. Cosmetic only, but EA-4 should note the discrepancy if cross-referencing the reports.

v3 handoff plan for `tools/gate_stale_cleaner/cleaner.py` says "VERIFY by reading the file: confirm no module-level REPO_ROOT, no hardcoded BlarAI paths, no direct load_config calls." Independent grep shows line 180 contains `cfg = config if config is not None else config_loader.load_config()` -- a fallback load_config call. EA-4 must disposition this fallback (either remove if config is always injected by callers, or thread ctx through to make the fallback project-aware). Today's production caller (run_live.py:58) always passes config so the fallback is dead path, but it is a load_config-callsite that should be addressed in 2.4 scope.

ack.topic: Legacy fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py disposition to 2.6 must close load_config callsite

plus_guide_5_acks.ack: v3 handoff defers `tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py` to Item 2.6 ("HTTP client has no project knowledge"). Independent grep confirms line 28 contains `cfg = config_loader.load_config()` (zero-arg). The defaults-preservation chain holds today via _resolve_project_overlay_path auto-resolve from BlarAI cwd. POST-Stage-4 cutover risk: if the script is invoked from devplatform cwd, the auto-resolve resolves to a non-BlarAI ctx and the overlay merge produces wrong allowlist. EA-4 MUST ensure the Item 2.6 disposition (whether "fold entirely" or "thin shim through new chokepoint") explicitly closes the line-28 load_config callsite before Item 2.9 single-commit. If 2.6 ends up leaving the load_config call in place without ctx threading, that is a w15 hazard and requires escalation back to Guide.

required_acknowledgments_carryforward_ve.ack_count_validation: 9 (critical_carryovers n=1..9) + 3 (g3-1..g3-3) + 4 (i1-1..i1-4) + 7 (g4-1..g4-7) + 4 (g5-1..g5-4) = 27. Matches Guide-#5 close-out: "All 23 binding acknowledgments from EA-3's iteration-2 gate (16 pre-existing + 7 Guide-#4) plus the 4 g5-N acknowledgments above remain in force through Stage 4 cutover (per i1-2 dedup rule)." Total = 23 + 4 = 27. ✓

comprehension_summary.part_a_objective: Resume Stage 2 (HIGH risk, single-session, multi-iteration gate) at Item 2.4 partial: refactor the remaining 7 of 13 Python entrypoint-side files to the canonical argparse + ProjectContext template proven across the 6 files EA-3 already refactored (uncommitted in working tree). After all 13 entrypoint files are pattern-clean, complete Item 2.4.v2 verification (--help on each + MCP handler routing), then Items 2.5 (7 PS1 with -BlarAIRoot) → 2.5.v2 → 2.6 (chokepoint grep + routing including g5-3 cleaner.py:180 and g5-4 legacy _vikunja_client.py:28 closures) → 2.7 / 2.7.v2 (V1-V7 + V8 + pytest ≥280) → 2.8 ($BLARAI_PID==3 hard gate) → 2.9 (single Stage-2 commit folding ALL refactor work including pyproject.toml; commit template per v3 handoff) → 2.10 (append-only STATUS.md Stage 2 closure entry). Defaults preserve byte-equivalent BlarAI behavior (V8) at every step. Branch chore/platform-extraction; no merge to main; fleet stays paused; stash@{0} untouched. If context tightens before 2.9 (likely candidate: file 13 vikunja_mcp/server.py MCP refactor — 19 handlers + project_id required-kwarg discipline), emit v4 mid-stage handoff XML per i1-4 (docs-only commit, no partial-commit refactor).

files_pending.file: Library-style refactor (no argparse). Add `project_context: "_project_context.ProjectContext | None" = None` kwarg to `run()`. When provided AND `config_path is None`, derive config_path = `ctx.root / "tools" / "autonomy_budget" / "config.yaml"`. Existing config_path / state_path / lifts_path kwargs (used by 5 tests) preserved. Line 76 `cfg = config_loader.load_config(config_path)`: keep positional; substitute derived path when config_path is None and project_context provided. Smallest lift; START HERE.

Constructor signature line 92: `repo_root: Path | str = Path(r"C:/Users/mrbla/BlarAI")`. Replace hardcoded default with `_project_context.DEFAULT_BLARAI_ROOT` (or sentinel + lazy resolve). Module-level `PROJECT_BLARAI_CORE_DEVELOPMENT: int = 3` flagged for likely Item 2.6 move to ctx.vikunja_project_id. Single-line default change + module constant decision.

Per ack g5-3: line 180 `cfg = config if config is not None else config_loader.load_config()` is a fallback load_config call (today dead path because run_live.py:58 always passes config). MUST disposition: either (a) remove fallback if always-injected invariant is enforced, or (b) thread ctx through to make fallback project-aware. Surface choice + rationale in 2.4 closure.
v3 handoff: "Already takes repo_root explicitly per existing tests; likely NO change. VERIFY by reading the file: confirm no module-level hardcoded BlarAI paths." Verify-only or trivial change.
v3 handoff: "Need to read. If module-level REPO_ROOT exists, replace with function-level resolution. If functions take repo_root explicitly already, no change." Verify-only or trivial change.

For Item 2.4: NO ctx-related change (HTTP client has no project knowledge). Per ack g5-4: the line-28 `cfg = config_loader.load_config()` (zero-arg) MUST be closed at Item 2.6 — either fold-entirely (delete LiveVikunjaClient + update ~6 callers) or thin-shim with explicit ctx threading. If 2.6 disposition leaves it open, escalate back to Guide before 2.9 commit (w15 hazard).

BIGGEST single-file lift in 2.4. Long-lived process (line 662 `if __name__ == "__main__":`), no argv control. Approach: (a) Resolve ProjectContext at startup via env-based `_project_context.resolve()` (honors BLARAI_PROJECT_ROOT). (b) Refactor 19 MCP handlers (list_projects, create_project, get_project, update_project, delete_project, list_tasks, create_task, get_task, update_task, complete_task, delete_task, list_labels, create_label, add_label_to_task, list_task_comments, add_task_comment, search_tasks, bulk_create_tasks, project_summary) to route through tools/_vikunja_client.VikunjaClient with _require_project_id guard. (c) CRITICAL FIX (Master Plan §D.1 violation): `list_tasks(project_id: int | None = None)` MUST become `list_tasks(*, project_id: int)`. Same for search_tasks and any other handler with project_id default. WARNING per v3: this single file may consume a focused successor session by itself; emit v4 handoff if context tightens before completing all 19 handlers.

item.pattern_template_to_apply: For files with main(): canonical EA-3-proven template (argparse + _build_parser() + ctx = _project_context.resolve(args.project_root, args.project_id) + load_config with explicit ctx-derived paths). For library files (7-12): kwarg-injected ctx with backwards-compat None default and auto-resolve fallback (D2 ratified). REPO_ROOT preservation per D1: keep module constant + add repo_root kwarg defaulting to REPO_ROOT for any helper with import-side callers.

item.title: Verify --help on all 12 Python entrypoints + MCP handler routing

part_b_work_plan.item: Per v3 handoff <item id="2.4.v2">. For each main()-bearing file: `python -m <module> --help` and confirm both --project-root and --project-id flags appear. For MCP server: spawn it and verify handlers route through chokepoint.

Canonical 7 (per iteration-2 gate, ratified): wake_launcher.ps1, agents-cadence-monitor.ps1, escalation_watchdog.ps1, toast_watchdog.ps1, la_merge_approve.ps1 (all under tools/scheduled-tasks/), escalation_notify.ps1 (tools/fleet_observability/), test_async_post_gate.ps1 (tools/scheduled-tasks/). Add -BlarAIRoot per g4-2 verbatim signature pattern. critical_notify.ps1 surfaced as audit-scope side effect (open question from iteration-2 carries forward — disposition to be decided in 2.5 closing handoff).

item.title: Verify each PS1 accepts -BlarAIRoot and defaults preserve BlarAI behavior
Vikunja chokepoint audit + replacement (extended grep per Guide-#5 / C6)

part_b_work_plan.item: Extended grep patterns per iteration-2 gate part_b. Triage every hit. MUST close ack g5-3 (cleaner.py:180 fallback) AND ack g5-4 (legacy _vikunja_client.py:28 load_config callsite) here. Also ack D3: dashboard_maintainer.py:113-117 live._request("POST", ...) private-method abuse.

item.title: V1-V7 closure assertions per EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml + V8 defaults preservation
$BLARAI_PID preflight (must equal 3 — STOP on drift per g4-5)
SINGLE Stage-2 commit (folding ALL items 2.1-2.8 work; pyproject.toml MUST be included)

part_b_work_plan.item: Commit template per v3 handoff <item id="2.9"><commit_template>.

item.title: Append-only STATUS.md Stage 2 closure entry (per g3-3 / ack n=9)

part_d_verification_strategy.per_python_entrypoint_v8: Run with no flags after refactor; confirm behavior matches pre-refactor baseline. Repeat for all 7 newly-refactored files (8-13 require full pattern; 7,10,11 may be verify-only).
part_d_verification_strategy.per_ps1_v8: At Item 2.5.v2: dot-source each PS1 with no args; confirm $BlarAIRoot default = BlarAI.
part_d_verification_strategy.mcp_handler_check: At Item 2.4.v2 (or as part of file 13 sub-completion): spawn MCP server; verify all 19 handlers route through tools/_vikunja_client.VikunjaClient with _require_project_id guard; verify list_tasks / search_tasks no longer accept project_id=None.
part_d_verification_strategy.cross_item_v1_v7: At 2.7 / 2.7.v2: V1=ProjectContext (9 tests, inherited), V2=VikunjaClient scope (6 tests, inherited), V3=overlay merge (6 tests, inherited), V4=CLI flag exposure (NEW parametrized per refactored entrypoint), V5=PS1 -BlarAIRoot (NEW), V6=MCP server project_id rejection (NEW mock-based), V7=cross-project byte-identity (NEW two-overlay diff).
part_d_verification_strategy.preflight_w3: At 2.8: $BLARAI_PID == 3 hard gate; STOP on drift per g4-5.
part_d_verification_strategy.commit_gate: Post-2.9: re-run pytest tools/; git status clean; commit hash captured for 2.10.

part_e_risks.risk: Existing files 7-13 may already define --project-root / --project-id; detect and reconcile rather than blindly add (none expected per v3 reading; verify per file).
Regression in any of 9 load_config callers (3 inherited + 6 already done + g5-3 fallback at cleaner.py:180); mitigated by explicit ctx-derived paths and V8 verification per file.
If file 13 vikunja_mcp/server.py 19 handlers don't all route through chokepoint, an LLM session can issue scope-leaking queries. HIGH-impact correctness risk; must validate every handler.

File 13 alone may consume the whole successor session. Will pre-allocate ~50% remaining budget to it; emit v4 handoff at first sign of strain (before opening any handler edit batch larger than I can complete + verify in remaining budget).
tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py vs tools/_vikunja_client.py — import-confusion + 2.6 disposition closure required for the line-28 load_config callsite; w15 hazard if missed.
Line 180 fallback is dead path today but a load_config callsite that must be dispositioned in 2.4 scope (not deferred).
live._request("POST", ...) at lines 113-117 left for 2.6 chokepoint disposition.
Item 2.6 must use the extended pattern set; narrow grep misses real call sites.
If 2.4 surfaces need for additional project-scoped config keys, extend _apply_project_overlay per-key (additive); never introduce generic deep-merge.
yaml type-stub lint warning is benign; do NOT add stubs as runtime dep.
Item 2.8 $BLARAI_PID must resolve to exactly 3; empty or other value -> STOP and report.
Fleet remains paused throughout (V0f confirmed); stash@{0} untouched (V0g confirmed); never call state.resume_fleet() (AttributeError reminder: function name is `resume_fleet`, NOT `unpause_fleet`).
If context exhausts before 2.9 -> emit v4 mid-stage handoff XML (docs-only commit), no partial-commit refactor. Same single-commit discipline holds.

11 M / 2 D / 6 ?? entries in V0c include LA-side housekeeping post EA-3 emission (GUIDE_PROMPT.xml +19, two D entries from "Temp for responses" rename, new lowercase temp_for_responses/ dir). NOT regressions; expected per ack g5-1.

comprehension_summary.part_f_rollback_acknowledgment: If post-item-2.9 verification fails (V1-V7 closure or per-item V8), rollback is `git reset --hard HEAD~1` on chore/platform-extraction (level-1 per RECOVERY.md; branch is local, not pushed). Branch is preserved for audit per copilot-instructions preservation_rule. Fleet stays disabled — no runtime impact from rollback.

ea_comprehension_gate.stop_discipline: STOPPING. Iteration-1 comprehension gate emitted with V0a-V0g pickup verification recap (all 7 PASS) and 27 binding acknowledgments restated verbatim (16 pre-existing + 7 g4-N + 4 g5-N). Awaiting Guide instance 5 review and explicit PROCEED authorization. NO item 2.4 work (file 7 self_check.py or any subsequent file), NO branch creation, NO mutation commands, NO state.resume_fleet() call until explicit authorization arrives.
ea_comprehension_gate.next_step_after_authorization: Begin Item 2.4 file 7: tools/autonomy_budget/self_check.py library-style refactor per v3 handoff <files_pending> entry. Smallest lift; proven canonical pattern already on disk in 6 EA-3 files (will read those end-to-end first per "mature not minimal" mandate before applying to file 7).
